bugs.title: CLAUDE.md state diagram shows active->planning transition but code rejects it
bugs.description: The CLAUDE.md state diagram shows 'Traveling -> Planning: Pause trip' as a valid transition, but isValidStatusTransition() in internal/trip/service.go only allows active->completed. The code has a comment marking completed as 'Terminal state' and active only transitions to completed. This is a documentation-vs-implementation discrepancy. The code behavior seems intentional (code comment says active->completed only), so the state diagram in CLAUDE.md should be updated to remove the active->planning arrow.
bugs.expected: Documentation and code agree on valid status transitions
bugs.actual: CLAUDE.md shows active->planning as valid, code returns failed_precondition for that transition
bugs.api_command: UpdateTrip with status TRIP_STATUS_PLANNING on an ACTIVE trip

ux_issues.description: DeleteBooking on non-existent ID returns empty success ({}) rather than NOT_FOUND. While idempotent delete is a valid design choice, it means clients cannot distinguish between 'successfully deleted' and 'never existed'. This could mask bugs where a client sends the wrong booking ID.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider returning NOT_FOUND for non-existent booking IDs, or at least include a 'deleted' boolean in the response to indicate whether a record was actually removed.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: This was a structural/adversarial test exercising error handling and validation, not a user experience test. All 10 test cases passed: buf.validate constraints correctly catch malformed IDs, empty strings, and oversized inputs; the status state machine properly rejects invalid transitions; ListTrips returns empty results rather than errors; and DeleteBooking is idempotent. The only findings are a P2 documentation discrepancy (CLAUDE.md state diagram vs code) and a P2 UX note about idempotent deletes.
